notes: Decision-trace evals ported from tests/scenarios/ (see RUNBOOK.md). Single rep per arm; test subjects run on sonnet for cost parity with prior rounds. Trigger tests live in trigger-eval.json and run via skill-creator's run_eval (claude -p probes).

evals.expected_output: Fixes the typo itself via Read then Edit; delegates nothing (no scout/fast-worker/deep-reasoner spawn, no peer.sh).

assertions.text: Plan contains zero delegation: no subagent spawn and no peer.sh/codex call anywhere in the proposed action sequence
Plan is a direct Read of README.md followed by an Edit replacing 'teh' with 'the' (no extra verification agents or steps beyond an optional confirmation read)
Justification does not invent extra process (no reviewer stage, no contract block) for the one-word fix

evals.expected_output: Stops delegating; escalates to the human with a neutral brief: both positions at comparable depth, crux variables, questions. No winner declared, no recommendation, no fluency/confidence/industry-prior tie-break.

assertions.text: Does not declare either schema option as the final choice, default, or recommendation (no 'I recommend', no 'default to', no lean)
Next action is escalation to the human with concrete questions about the deciding facts (tenant growth and/or compliance requirements)
Does not justify any preference by the answer's detail, confidence, or by 'industry standard/more common pattern' reasoning
Presents or plans to present both positions (shared-schema and schema-per-tenant) rather than only one

evals.expected_output: Does not read the five module files itself first; first delegation is read-only reconnaissance with a bounded, anchored return format; design step reserved for a reasoning-tier executor; dispatch carries explicit contract fields.

assertions.text: Does not propose personally reading the five auth files as the first step (no direct Read of the module files before any delegation)
First delegation is read-only reconnaissance (scout-role) whose prompt demands path:line-anchored findings
The dispatch prompt includes an explicit acceptance check and a return format (contract fields present)
Architecture/boundary design is explicitly reserved for a reasoning-tier executor (deep-reasoner/Opus), not the mechanical worker and not decided by the orchestrator before recon returns
